== Backlog
// This chapter lists proposed items for the xlsx-backend refactor & adjacent features.
// Keep this lightweight and link to per-item FTR files later if needed.
=== Open
* FTR-IR-READPATH — XLSX read via IR
** Implement the mirror of the write pipeline: Excel→IR→Frames(+Meta).
** Acceptance: roundtrip JSON→XLSX→JSON equality (ignoring presentation-only meta).
* FTR-MULTIHEADER — Multi-row/column headers (with merges)
** Composer support for row/col header levels; renderer applies merges and styles.
** Acceptance: headers render as specified; data offsets correct; tests cover both axes.
* FTR-COMPACT-TRANSFORMS — Xref ↔ Cross-table ↔ Compact string (E-V-Z) (bijective)
** Provide two bijective steps: (1) xref⇄cross-table; (2) stack multiple cross-tables into compact codes using per-table code + separator.
** Acceptance: lossless roundtrip across all three forms; separator + mapping fully meta-driven.
* FTR-VALIDATION-COMPACT — Compact code validation generator (with cap)
** Generate allowed dropdown values as all presence/absence combinations across participating layers (order preserved),
with a hard cap (e.g., 2,000) to avoid unusable Excel lists.
** Acceptance: lists generated; capped with clear warning; applied via DataValidation.
* FTR-META-PERSISTENCE — Persist Meta in XLSX and JSON
** XLSX: hidden _meta sheet + named anchors; JSON: optional sidecar `<sheet>._meta.yaml` for read/write.
** Acceptance: meta roundtrips and is ignored by pure-data adapters unless enabled.
* FTR-JSON-BUNDLER — Nested JSON support via synthetic sheets + named ranges
** Split lists of objects into synthetic tables on the same sheet (or dedicated sheets) and bundle back to nested JSON.
** Acceptance: JSON→Sheets→JSON structural identity; links recorded in meta; namespaced named ranges.
* FTR-PARTITION-BY-DOMAIN — Partition/merge by domain component attribute
** Split/merge frames by a mutually-exclusive ‘component’ attribute (lookup via join on variable) to reduce merge conflicts.
** Acceptance: deterministic partition layout; recomposition identical; configurable pre-persist step.
* FTR-LEGEND-BLOCKS — Legend/Config blocks & multiple tables per sheet
** Composer places legend/config as TableBlocks alongside data (no table explosion).
** Acceptance: blocks positioned by top-left coordinates; styling distinct; documented in meta.
* FTR-FORMULA-PROVIDERS — Formula DSL & FK linking
** DSL to generate stable formulas (e.g., XLOOKUP) using named ranges, not A1 literals; renderer translates to Excel/ODS.
** Acceptance: references resolve correctly; no hard-coded coordinates; tests assert formula strings.
* FTR-STYLE-THEMES — Themes & helper band highlighting
** StylePass applies theme tokens (headers, helpers, data); configurable colors and freeze/autofilter.
** Acceptance: visual diff stable; helper columns/rows clearly marked; options in config.
* FTR-ODS-RENDERER — Parity adapter for OpenDocument Spreadsheet
** Implement ODS renderer using the same IR; gracefully degrade unsupported features.
** Acceptance: core layout renders; fallbacks documented; tests assert no crashes.
* FTR-TEST-HARNESS — Dual-backend markers & CI targets
** `@pytest.mark.xlsx_ir` and `@pytest.mark.xlsx_legacy`; Make targets `test-ir`, `test-legacy`, default excludes IR-only.
** Acceptance: CI can run legacy-only, IR-only, and full suites; flakes eliminated.
* FTR-PERF-LIMITS — Safety guards for combinatorial lists
** Hard cap for compact permutations and large validation sources; log warnings in meta/issues.
** Acceptance: runs do not blow up memory/Excel; user feedback present.
* FTR-NAMED-RANGES — Systematic naming for blocks & data areas
** Auto-generate stable names per TableBlock and per data area (header/data ranges).
** Acceptance: names unique, deterministic, and used by formulas/validations.
* FTR-COLUMN-MAP — Validation targeting by actual column
** Map validations to the correct column indices based on header inference from Composer.
** Acceptance: dropdowns appear in the intended column(s); header changes update areas.
* FTR-META-SCHEMA-V2 — Consolidate meta (compact_maps, separator, component, validations)
** Define/adopt a schema for: compact layer codes, separator, validation specs, domain component mapping, legend blocks.
** Acceptance: schema documented; validated at load; used uniformly across passes.
=== In Progress
* FTR-IR-WRITEPATH — (DONE in code, refining) IR write pipeline with openpyxl renderer
** Current status: basic write path wired; minimal list validation present; more features to follow.
=== Done
* FTR-DEV-MARKERS — Introduce xlsx_ir / xlsx_legacy markers, Make targets
** Status: merged; developers can run legacy or IR selectively.
